query: Roman spaghetti carbonara with guanciale, eggs, pecorino romano, and black pepper

draft.title: Spaghetti Carbonara
draft.description: Classic Roman spaghetti carbonara with guanciale, eggs, pecorino romano, and black pepper, perfect for a rich and savory meal.

steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente, about 8-9 minutes.
While pasta cooks, heat a pan over medium heat and cook the guanciale until crisp and golden, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, grated pecorino romano, and freshly ground black pepper until smooth.
Reserve about 150 ml of pasta cooking water, then drain the spaghetti.
Add the hot spaghetti to the pan with guanciale off the heat. Quickly pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta, tossing vigorously to create a creamy sauce without scrambling the eggs.
Add reserved pasta water a little at a time if needed to loosen the sauce and achieve a silky texture.
Serve immediately with extra pecorino and black pepper if desired.

warnings: Ensure eggs are added off the heat to prevent scrambling.
Use fresh pecorino romano and guanciale for authentic flavor.
